data.title: Maggie Nolan Reiterates Buy on Cognizant as Large Deals, Acquisitions and Project Leap Drive Earnings and Margin Upside Potential
data.description:
William Blair analyst Maggie Nolan has maintained their bullish stance on CTSH stock, giving a Buy rating yesterday.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Maggie Nolan has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Cognizant’s underlying momentum and improving profitability profile. While the first quarter showed only modest revenue growth and a slight margin shortfall, the company delivered solid EPS and benefited from strong large-deal activity, particularly in North America and financial services, which supports longer-term revenue visibility.

Management’s outlook for the second half anticipates acceleration as large contracts scale, recent acquisitions contribute, and discretionary demand gradually improves, offsetting near-term caution embedded in guidance. In addition, the cost-efficiency initiatives under Project Leap are expected to drive meaningful, recurring savings and margin expansion over the next few years, giving Nolan confidence that earnings growth and cash generation can outpace current expectations and justify a Buy rating despite a mixed near-term setup.

According to TipRanks, Nolan is an analyst with an average return of -10.3% and a 34.46% success rate. Nolan covers the Technology sector, focusing on stocks such as Exlservice Holdings, Accenture, and Cognizant.

In another report released yesterday, J.P. Morgan also assigned a Buy rating to the stock with a $74.00 price target.

data.title: Analyst Reiterates Buy Rating on Ionis Pharmaceuticals, Maintains $105 Price Target on Strong Q1 and Upgraded Olezarsen Outlook
data.description:
In a report released today, Joseph Stringer from Needham maintained a Buy rating on Ionis Pharmaceuticals, with a price target of $105.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Joseph Stringer has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including Ionis Pharmaceuticals’ strong first-quarter performance, where revenue exceeded both his projections and Street expectations, largely on the back of higher-than-anticipated R&D-related income. This outperformance allowed management to lift full-year 2026 revenue guidance while narrowing expected non-GAAP operating losses, signaling improved financial visibility and operational execution.

In addition, Stringer highlights an upgraded peak sales outlook for olezarsen in severe hypertriglyceridemia, now projected to surpass $3 billion, which he views as establishing a solid valuation floor for the shares. He believes this foundation creates an attractive risk/reward profile, especially with several important late-stage catalysts on the horizon, including Phase 3 data for eplontersen in ATTR-CM and pelacarsen in Lp(a) led by Novartis, and he therefore reiterates his $105 price target.

According to TipRanks, Stringer is a 5-star analyst with an average return of 24.1% and a 46.95% success rate. Stringer covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as Gilead Sciences, Ionis Pharmaceuticals, and Rhythm Pharmaceuticals.

data.title: Bausch + Lomb: Solid Q1 Beat and Pharma Momentum Offset by Spin-Off Uncertainty, Supporting Hold Rating
data.description:
Needham analyst David Saxon has maintained their neutral stance on BLCO stock, giving a Hold rating on April 23.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

David Saxon has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors tied to both operating performance and corporate structure. He acknowledges that first-quarter 2026 revenue exceeded expectations, helped in part by favorable foreign exchange, and that EBITDA also surpassed consensus. He also notes that the pharmaceuticals division outperformed, with Xiidra demonstrating stronger-than-anticipated momentum and now projected to deliver low double-digit growth instead of the previously assumed mid-single-digit pace.

At the same time, Saxon highlights that year-over-year margin expansion, supported by disciplined SG&A spending, points to improving efficiency across the business. Despite this constructive start to the year, he keeps a neutral stance because there is still insufficient clarity around Bausch + Lomb’s planned separation from its parent, Bausch Health Companies. This structural uncertainty tempers the otherwise positive fundamentals, leading him to maintain a Hold rather than move to a more decisive rating.

In another report released on April 23, Barclays also reiterated a Hold rating on the stock with a $20.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Hannes Leitner Raises Price Target to $22.2 but Maintains Hold on Concerns Over Sustainability of Growth Drivers
data.description:
In a report released today, Hannes Leitner from Jefferies maintained a Hold rating on EDENRED, with a price target of €22.20.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Hannes Leitner has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors, including first‑quarter revenues that surpassed expectations thanks to stronger‑than‑anticipated savings on rebates and solid other revenues supported by elevated float and interest rates. While this demonstrates effective short‑term mitigation of the regulatory headwinds in Italy, it also highlights that part of the outperformance is tied to a favorable rate environment that may not be sustainable over the longer term.

Looking ahead, he expects regulatory countermeasures, higher face values, increased fuel prices and disciplined cost management to support EBITDA growth through 2026, leading him to lift his estimates above the company’s 8–12% guidance range and to raise his price target from $18.2 to $22.2. However, lingering concerns about the quality and durability of the underlying growth drivers, once temporary benefits fade, justify maintaining a neutral stance and therefore a Hold recommendation on the shares.

In another report released on April 24, Barclays also maintained a Hold rating on the stock with a €21.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Parsons Earns Buy Rating on Resilient Growth, Record Backlog, and Reaffirmed Outlook Despite Middle East Headwinds
data.description:
William Blair analyst Louie DiPalma has reiterated their bullish stance on PSN stock, giving a Buy rating today.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Louie DiPalma has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Parsons’ resilient fundamentals and outlook. He notes that despite recent pressure from concerns about Middle East megaproject slowdowns, management has already incorporated these reallocations into guidance, and new data center, transportation, and modernization work is helping offset reductions.

He also points to solid organic growth in both federal solutions and critical infrastructure, supported by strong demand in cyber, missile defense, transportation, and broader global infrastructure. Margin improvement, record backlog, and a book-to-bill of 1.4 times across segments underpin confidence in durable growth, while management’s reaffirmed full-year outlook and absence of operational disruptions in the Middle East further validate the bullish stance.

DiPalma covers the Technology sector, focusing on stocks such as Palantir Technologies, Motorola Solutions, and ViaSat. According to TipRanks, DiPalma has an average return of 3.5% and a 51.72% success rate on recommended stocks.

data.title: Spyre Therapeutics: Buy Rating Reaffirmed as External Combo Data Support IBD Strategy; $95 Price Target Unchanged
data.description:
Analyst Debanjana Chatterjee from JonesTrading reiterated a Buy rating on Spyre Therapeutics and keeping the price target at $95.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Debanjana Chatterjee has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to strengthening external validation for Spyre Therapeutics’ inflammatory bowel disease strategy. Recent proof-of-concept data from AbbVie’s α4β7/IL-23p19 combination in difficult-to-treat Crohn’s patients showed substantially better endoscopic remission than either monotherapy, supporting the broader view that dual-pathway combinations can deliver markedly superior outcomes in this setting.

Chatterjee argues that Spyre’s purpose-built co-formulations are well positioned to capitalize on this trend, offering potential advantages in convenience, pricing, safety and target engagement versus emerging rivals. The expected quarterly or longer dosing schedule could be more attractive than competitors’ monthly regimens, and if Spyre can price its combinations near the cost of a single biologic, adoption earlier in the treatment algorithm becomes more likely. With external combo data aligning with Spyre’s thesis and its development timeline tracking closely with large peers, the unchanged $95 price target and Buy rating are maintained.

In another report released today, LifeSci Capital also assigned a Buy rating to the stock with a $105.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Teva Upgraded to $45 Price Target on Strong Execution, Emalex Acquisition, and Growing Neurology Pipeline Upside
data.description:
Truist Financial analyst Les Sulewski maintained a Buy rating on Teva Pharmaceutical today and set a price target of $45.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Les Sulewski has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Teva’s solid execution and strengthening growth profile. The company’s first-quarter performance reinforced confidence in management’s ability to deliver on its strategic pivot toward innovative medicines, prompting him to raise his price target to $45 as the outlook for earnings and pipeline value improves.

He also views the Emalex acquisition as a highly attractive catalyst, adding a late-stage Tourette syndrome asset with potential peak sales approaching $1 billion and meaningful U.S. market exclusivity. In his view, ecopipam’s differentiated mechanism, favorable Phase 3 data, and ability to address a major unmet need position Teva for significant upside in neurology, while the broader pipeline, including its anti–IL‑15 program, offers additional optionality and justifies a continued bullish stance.

In another report released on April 23, Piper Sandler also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $41.00 price target.

Andrew Charles has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Yum! Brands’ operating momentum and Taco Bell’s outperformance. He highlights the first-quarter earnings per share coming in ahead of expectations, driven largely by Taco Bell’s strong same-store sales and effective execution across value offerings, beverages, marketing, and operations, which support an upward revision of Taco Bell’s long-term sales and margin outlook.

In addition, Charles emphasizes that management’s comments eased worries about geopolitical issues slowing KFC’s international expansion, reinforcing confidence in Yum!’s global development story. data.title: GE Healthcare Kept at Hold as Analyst Cuts Price Target to $65 on Margin Pressure and Execution Risks
data.description:
GE Healthcare Technologies Inc, the Healthcare sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst today. Analyst Joanne Wuensch from Citi maintained a Hold rating on the stock and has a $65.00 price target.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Joanne Wuensch has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors tied to GE Healthcare’s latest quarter and outlook. The company modestly exceeded revenue expectations, but meaningful margin compression and an earnings shortfall, driven in part by weakness in PCS and a profit hit in PDx, undermined the quality of the beat and signaled execution issues.

At the same time, management cut its 2026 margin outlook and reduced the price target from $80 to $65, reflecting a tougher inflation backdrop and external pressures such as geopolitical tensions. However, steady mid‑single‑digit organic growth guidance, expectations for PCS to improve in the second half, and resolving supply issues in PDx prevent a more negative stance, leaving the shares in a “show‑me” phase that supports a Neutral, or Hold, recommendation.

Wuensch covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as Boston Scientific, GE Healthcare Technologies Inc, and Edwards Lifesciences. According to TipRanks, Wuensch has an average return of 0.8% and a 50.00% success rate on recommended stocks.
